_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to utilize LoTL TTP (i.e., pivoting post initial access) in an operational setting.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data serialization formats and their trade-offs for different workloads, including JSON, Apache Avro, Apache Parquet, and Protocol Buffers, with understanding of schema evolution, compression characteristics, and query performance implications.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-003 (LS, cosine: 0.351), AI-K-009 (AI, cosine: 0.347).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.3/10 (raw 0.132). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, access, i, initial, lotl, operational] vs WIDAI-only terms [apache, avro, buffers, characteristics, compression, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.836 (P=0.842, R=0.830). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 31-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Ability to utilize LoTL TTP (i.e., pivoting post initial access) in an operation" and "Knowledge of data serialization formats and their trade-offs for different workl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.394 > STS 0.132 (gap +0.262). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, access, i, initial, lotl, operational]. WIDAI terms: [apache, avro, buffers, characteristics, compression, data].
